title: Move zeroization gaps to module-level docs (not inline comments)
description: The previous code had '// ZEROIZATION GAP' comments inline inside get() and put() explaining why secret bytes linger. The refactor moved this to a module-level '# Zeroization' rustdoc section. This is the right call: the limitation is architectural (the vaultrs library), not a detail of any specific line. Inline comments at the vaultrs call sites would mislead a contributor into thinking the gap could be fixed by changing those lines. A module doc section makes it visible to crate users reading docs.rs. Defend this if a contributor suggests reinstating the inline comments.
